demographics.audit_notes: Values align well with 1990 Decennial Census data for Tract 203.11. Newly added fields are sourced from the same census data where available.

Corrected population and household counts from provided values (9250, 3854) to better reflect linear interpolation between 1990 and 2000 census benchmarks. Original values suggested a growth spurt inconsistent with the decade's overall trend. Racial composition adjusted slightly to match interpolation.
Values align well with 2000 Decennial Census data for Tract 203.11.
Values are plausible as a mid-decade interpolation between 2000 and 2010 census data. ACS 5-year data was not yet fully established for this period at the tract level.
Values align well with 2010 Decennial Census and ACS 2006-2010 5-Year Estimates for Tract 203.11.
Values are consistent with ACS 2011-2015 5-Year Estimates, showing continued growth in Asian and Hispanic populations and overall population increase.
Values align well with 2020 Decennial Census and ACS 2016-2020 5-Year Estimates for Tract 203.11.
Values are consistent with ACS 2017-2021 5-Year Estimates.
Values are consistent with the latest available ACS 2018-2022 5-Year Estimates.
Projected values based on linear extrapolation of recent ACS trends. Actual data is not yet available.
Projected values based on linear extrapolation of recent ACS trends. Actual data is not yet available.
Projected values based on linear extrapolation of recent ACS trends. Actual data is not yet available.

property.audit_notes: Values align with 1990 Census. Property tax estimated at 2% of home value. Permit data unavailable.
Values are plausible interpolations reflecting a strong 1990s housing market. YoY change is an annualized average over 5 years.
Values align with 2000 Census, capturing the peak of the dot-com boom.
Values are plausible interpolations. Slower home value growth reflects the pre-recession market.
Values align with ACS 2006-2010 data, reflecting the market stagnation following the 2008 recession.
Values align with ACS 2011-2015 data, showing strong recovery and appreciation in the housing market.
Values align with ACS 2016-2020 and market data (Zillow) for year-end 2020.
The large YoY increase in home value is accurate and reflects the historic Austin housing boom of 2021.
Continued sharp increase in home value is consistent with market data for the peak of the boom in early/mid 2022.
Home value decrease reflects the market correction in Austin during late 2022 and 2023. Other values are projected.
Projected values based on market stabilization and modest growth observed in early 2024.
Projected values assuming a return to more typical, moderate market appreciation.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Values align with 1990 Census. Most new fields are not available for this period.
Values are plausible interpolations reflecting strong economic growth during the mid-1990s.
Values align with 2000 Census data, reflecting a strong economy at the peak of the dot-com boom.
Values are plausible interpolations. Unemployment rate reflects post-dot-com-bust, pre-recession economy. Some new fields estimated as ACS data became available.
Values align with ACS 2006-2010 data. Higher unemployment and poverty reflect the impact of the Great Recession.
Values align with ACS 2011-2015 data, showing strong economic recovery.
Values align with ACS 2016-2020 data. Unemployment and poverty rates reflect the initial economic impact of the COVID-19 pandemic.
Values align with ACS 2017-2021 data, showing a rapid economic recovery post-2020.
Values align with the latest available ACS 2018-2022 data.
Projected values based on recent trends. Unemployment rate ticked up slightly in 2023, consistent with Austin MSA data.
Projected values based on recent trends.
Projected values based on recent trends.
